nits: Spec text says "scale from 50 to ~500 polls per minute" but with POLL_BATCH_SIZE bumped to 100 + POLL_MAX_CHAIN_DEPTH=5, the actual ceiling is 6×100=600/min — code comment is correct, but the AUTOMATIONS-PLAN.md H.4 paragraph still reads "~500" and is now stale.

H.3 per-workspace cap (5) combined with H.4 chain trigger means a single workspace with 1000 due rows is rate-limited to 5/min regardless of chain depth (the chain only fires when scanned===BATCH_SIZE, which requires multiple workspaces contributing); intentional fairness but worth a doc note.

Self-invoke failure is logged non-fatal and remaining rows stay leased for TENTATIVE_LEASE_SECONDS=300s, so a single failed invoke creates a ~4-minute window where leftover rows aren't reprocessed by subsequent EB ticks — not data loss but worth noting in runbook.

H.6 reservedConcurrentExecutions=10 is not yet deployed; until then, an unlucky pile-up of EB ticks + chain hops can spawn more concurrent kicker instances than the planned cap — bounded by account-level Lambda concurrency (1000 default).

Test file's lambdaSendMock at module-top is hoisted; the explicit lambdaSendMock.mockClear() in beforeEach is correct, but if a future test uses vi.doMock on @aws-sdk/client-lambda the restoration discipline from the parallel-sweeps test must be replicated — currently fine, just a maintenance footgun.

InvokeCommand payload uses Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(...)); works because Buffer extends Uint8Array, but AWS SDK v3 docs recommend new TextEncoder().encode(...) for cross-runtime portability — purely stylistic.
